# Architectural Drawing Description

This is a historical architectural drawing on aged, cream-colored paper featuring detailed construction plans for a Victorian-era building. The main composition displays a longitudinal section view showing the full side profile of a substantial multi-story structure with varied rooflines and architectural elements. The building features a prominent tall tower with a pointed spire on the left side, multiple pitched roofs with decorative gables, numerous windows arranged in regular patterns across multiple levels, and an ornate base with columns and railings. A grand entrance staircase is visible on the lower left. Two smaller detail drawings appear above the main section: one showing a cross-sectional view labeled "Transverse Section" and another showing what appears to be a roof detail view. The drawing includes numerous handwritten measurements and annotations in light brown ink throughout, with label text reading "Longitudinal Section" at the bottom and references to "East Elevation" and other directional indicators. Additional scale measurements and construction notes are written at the base of the drawing. A color scale bar appears along the right edge. The entire composition demonstrates professional architectural draftsmanship typical of 19th-century building design documentation.

# Architectural Floor Plan Description

This is a historic architectural blueprint showing the second floor of a multi-story building, drawn on aged cream-colored paper with visible stains and wear. At the top of the document is a color reference scale with bands of navy blue, cyan, green, yellow, magenta, and dark gray.

The floor plan features detailed room layouts drawn with brown and tan ink lines, showing multiple bedrooms labeled "Bed Room" with various dimensions listed in feet and inches. The layout is symmetrical, with rooms arranged around central hallway spaces and staircases marked with hatched patterns. Diagonal lines in several rooms indicate windows or architectural features.

In the upper portion of the plan, there are two larger rooms labeled "Adjoining Bed Room" with dimensions of approximately 18'6" x 20'6". The middle section shows smaller rooms and hallways, including spaces labeled "Bath Room" with dimensions like 11'2" x 14". The lower section displays numerous additional bed rooms and rooms labeled with numbers in parentheses, arranged in rows with consistent dimensions.

The notation at the bottom reads "SECOND FLOOR" and indicates "SCALED EIGHT FEET TO ONE INCH." The architect's credit "F. McILLUS & SON, ARCHTS." appears at the bottom right, along with an address "330 PINE ST S.F."
